title: phelipanche purpurea subsp. bohemica
intro: Phelipanche purpurea subsp. bohemica, also known as purple broomrape, is a parasitic flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Phelipanche purpurea subsp. bohemica include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of various ailments.
